ops.title: hookd daemon conventions inventory (reference impl for docs/conventions.md)
ops.body:
Cited inventory of captain-hook's Python daemon (hookd) as-implemented, gathered by a codex/gpt-5.6-sol xhigh recon on 2026-07-18. Feeds daemonkit's `docs/conventions.md` (hookd + Captain Hook = the non-Go reference impls). Repo: /Users/yasyf/Code/captain-hook, daemon under `captain_hook/daemon/`, thin client + spawn in `capt_hook_client/`.

**1. Wire envelope.** `PROTOCOL = 1` shared both sides (`capt_hook_client/key.py:22`). Op discriminator field is `kind`, NOT `op` (`daemon/protocol.py:19-36`). Kinds: `event`, `ping`, `status`, `drain`, `shutdown` (last four = control) (`protocol.py:69-72`). Request `{"v", "kind", "client":{"version","build","pid","ppid"}}` + optional `event`/`async`/`root`/`cwd`/`hooks`/`env`/`payload_raw`; no runtime validation of optionals, extra keys tolerated (`protocol.py:99-109,130-154`). Response `{"v","daemon":{version,build,pid},"status":"ok|error|rejected","stdout","stderr","exit","elapsed_ms"}` all fields always emitted (`protocol.py:113-120,157-175`). Framing: one request per connection, one newline-terminated JSON each way (`protocol.py:1-8,178-193`). MAX_LINE = **32 MiB** (`protocol.py:63-67`) — NOTE: plan §2 said capt-hook = 64 KiB; the event daemon is actually 32 MiB. 3s server read deadline; malformed/over-limit closed with no response (`server.py:74-77,207-214`).

**2. Socket + lock.** Worker key = first 16 hex of SHA-256(project-root realpath + sorted worker-partition env, NUL-joined) (`key.py:46-53`). Run dir `$CAPT_HOOK_RUN_DIR` / `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/captain-hook/run` / `~/.cache/...`; socket `<key>.sock`, lock `<key>.lock` (`key.py:56-76`); sun_path 104-byte bound enforced (`protocol.py:63-66,123-127`). Spawn race: run-dir 0700, lock `O_CREAT|O_RDWR` 0600, `flock(LOCK_EX|LOCK_NB)`; EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK/EACCES = loser, polls connect+flock every 25ms to deadline (`client.py:282-305`). Lock FD held across stale-socket removal/spawn/readiness, closed only in finally; no explicit LOCK_UN/unlink; daemon Popen `close_fds=True` so never inherits flock (`client.py:282-337`). Stale socket: winner rechecks connectivity, unlinks+spawns only if dead; server on bind probes existing socket, exits 0 if live else unlinks+binds; refuses symlinked run dir, chmod dir 0700/socket 0600 (`server.py:404-422,592-605`).

**3. Spawn + readiness.** Single `Popen` (NO double fork) of `python -m captain_hook daemon run --root <root>` (`client.py:321-337`). Detach `start_new_session=True`, `close_fds=True`, stdin DEVNULL, stdout/stderr → append-only 0600 boot log `O_NOFOLLOW`; no env/cwd/pass_fds. Readiness is connection-based, no ping handshake: winner polls `child.poll()` + one-shot connect every 10ms; early child exit = pre-send failure (`client.py:307-319`). Every connection checked against peer kernel UID; mismatch closed untrusted (`client.py:241-279`). Deadline 30s default / 20s UserPromptSubmit, override `CAPT_HOOK_CLIENT_TIMEOUT`.

**4. Idle exit.** Default 2h (120*60s), override `HOOKS_DAEMON_IDLE_S` (`lifecycle.py:29-33,70-75`). `last_activity` updated on accepted connection (pre-read) AND on successful decode — intake activity, not dispatch completion (`server.py:198-218`). Checked on accept() 2s timeout. Veto = only `active > 0` after limit; no session/cache/queue veto (`server.py:462-470`). Idle → `_shutdown(restart=False)`: unlink socket, drain accepted backlog, NO self-reexec (`server.py:471-513`).

**5. Version/skew.** `v` = protocol int (1). `client.version` always `""` from both thin+ops clients. Daemon version from installed metadata. Package version strings NEVER compared — server gate is only `req.v == PROTOCOL` (`server.py:325-335`); client validates response v/status/exit, never `daemon.version` (`client.py:373-389`). Protocol mismatch → `status:"rejected"`, no shutdown/restart; client raises BadResponse (post-send), event handling swallows → exit 0 no retry. Build fingerprint: thin client `CAPT_HOOK_CLIENT_BUILD` or `<mtime_ns>-<size>`; daemon build = version or version+digest over both packages (checkout) (`lifecycle.py:36-61`). Build skew: first event records build; watchdog recomputes daemon build every 2s (2s TTL), needs TWO consecutive mismatches → restart = daemon self-`os.execv`s recorded argv (`lifecycle.py:108-133`) — daemon-initiated, NOT client-spawn.

**6. Health/shutdown ops.** ALL kinds behind the same gate: decode success then `req.v == PROTOCOL` — NO pre-gate lifecycle op; even ping/status/drain/shutdown sit behind the protocol check (`server.py:207-214,325-335`). NOTE: plan claims Health/Shutdown should answer BEFORE the proto gate (cc-interact's rule); hookd VIOLATES this too, so it is not a pre-gate reference. `ping`→empty ok; `status`→ok+JSON(pid/build/version/root/uptime); `drain`+`shutdown` wire-identical → reply ok then `_shutdown(restart=False)`. CLI: `hookd status` uses ping for liveness; `hookd restart` sends `drain` (daemon just exits, next event respawns — no reexec); `hookd stop` sends `shutdown`. OOB signals: SIGTERM=shutdown, SIGHUP=drop caches, SIGUSR1=dump stacks (`server.py:158-161`).

**7. Client error taxonomy.** `DaemonUnavailable`(pre-send; subclass `UntrustedWorker`), `DeadlineExpired`(pre-send), `PostSendFailure`(after sendall; subclass `BadResponse`) (`client.py:56-76`). Pre-send: any connect/spawn/send OSError→DaemonUnavailable even on partial write; untrusted run dir/foreign UID always cold path regardless of fallback. Post-send: read errors/EOF/wait-deadline→PostSendFailure; malformed/non-object/v-mismatch/bad-status/bad-exit→BadResponse. Outcomes: ok/error emitted verbatim (error NOT rerun cold); same-protocol `rejected` → fallback policy; event post-send failure → breadcrumb + exit 0, no cold rerun. `CAPT_HOOK_DAEMON_FALLBACK`: open→0, closed→1, default cold→run `python -m captain_hook` once. Retries: initial connect exactly 3× 25ms apart; losers poll to deadline; winner polls 10ms.

**Two plan corrections surfaced:** (a) MAX_LINE is 32 MiB not 64 KiB; (b) hookd puts ALL ops behind the protocol gate, so it is NOT a Health/Shutdown-pre-gate reference — cc-interact remains the only pre-gate exemplar.
